perturbation.original_text: 7. RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION If requested in writing by Discloser , Recipient must cease using, return to Discloser and/or destroy all Confidential Information and any copies of Confidential Information in its possessi on or control. Recipient may retain one archival copy of such Confidential Information for the sole purpose of establishing the extent of the disclosure of such Confidential Information, p rovided that such information is not used by Recipient for any other purpose and is subject to the confident iality requirements set out in this Agreement .
perturbation.changed_text: 7. RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION If requested in writing by Discloser , Recipient may, at its discretion, cease using, return to Discloser and/or destroy all Confidential Information and any copies of Confidential Information in its possessi on or control, unless retaining such information is required for ongoing business operations. Recipient may retain multiple copies of such Confidential Information for various internal purposes, provided such information is used by Recipient only in a non-competitive manner and is not subject to confidentiality requirements set out in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'must cease using, return, and/or destroy' to 'may, at its discretion, cease using, return, and/or destroy' and allowing 'multiple copies' for 'various internal purposes', directly contradicts the obligation to return or destroy the information. Removing the obligation to keep the archival copy confidential introduces a conflict. The term 'non-competitive manner' is vague, making enforcement difficult if commercial use arises. This weakens the protection of Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION If requested in writing by Discloser , Recipient may, at its discretion, cease using, return to Discloser and/or destroy all Confidential Information and any copies of Confidential Information in its possessi on or control, unless retaining such information is required for ongoing business operations. Recipient may retain multiple copies of such Confidential Information for various internal purposes, provided such information is used by Recipient only in a non-competitive manner and is not subject to confidentiality requirements set out in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated the return or destruction of confidential information upon request. The changed text grants the Recipient discretion in returning or destroying the information, allows for retaining multiple copies for internal purposes, removes the confidentiality requirement on retained copies, and introduces a vague 'non-competitive manner' restriction. This directly contradicts the original intent and the implied requirement for consistent handling of confidential information throughout the agreement, making enforcement unclear. The definition for Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction applies, as the obligations are defined inconsistently within the contract.

perturbation.original_text: 9. LIMITED WARRANTY & LIABILITY Discloser warrants that it has the right to disclose the Confidential Information to Recipient. Discl oser makes no other warranties in respect of the Confidential Information and provides all information “AS IS” without any express or implied warranty of any kind, including any warrant y as to merchantability, fitness for a particular purpo se, accu racy, co mpleteness or violation of third party intellectual property rights. Neither party will be liable for any special, incidental or consequential damages of any kind whatsoever resulting from the disclosure, use or receipt of the Confidential Information .
perturbation.changed_text: 9. LIMITED WARRANTY & LIABILITY Discloser warrants that it has the right to disclose the Confidential Information to Recipient, only to the extent that such right has not been previously assigned. Discl oser makes warranties in respect of the Confidential Information and provides all information with a warranty of reasonable accuracy and completeness. Both parties will be liable for any special, incidental or consequential damages of any kind whatsoever resulting from the disclosure, use or receipt of the Confidential Information, up to the value of $10,000.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'makes no other warranties' to 'makes warranties...reasonable accuracy and completeness' introduces a direct contradiction. Also changing from 'Neither party will be liable' to 'Both parties will be liable', adds to the contradiction. Adding 'only to the extent that such right has not been previously assigned' creates another level of liability uncertainty. This contradiction introduces ambiguity about the warranty status and who bears liability for damages, therefore complicating dispute resolution.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. LIMITED WARRANTY & LIABILITY Discloser warrants that it has the right to disclose the Confidential Information to Recipient, only to the extent that such right has not been previously assigned. Discl oser makes warranties in respect of the Confidential Information and provides all information with a warranty of reasonable accuracy and completeness. Both parties will be liable for any special, incidental or consequential damages of any kind whatsoever resulting from the disclosure, use or receipt of the Confidential Information, up to the value of $10,000.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states 'makes no other warranties' and 'Neither party will be liable'. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating 'makes warranties...reasonable accuracy and completeness' and 'Both parties will be liable'. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text. This creates a direct conflict regarding warranties and liability, leading to ambiguity and potential disputes, thus a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 11.6 Entire Agreement – This Agreement represents the enti re agreement between the parties with regard to the Con fidential Inform ation and supersedes any previous understandings, commitments or agreements, whether written or oral. No amendment or modification of this Agreement will be effective unless made in wri ting and signed by authorized representatives of both p arties.
perturbation.changed_text: 11.6 Entire Agreement – This Agreement represents only a partial understanding between the parties with regard to the Con fidential Inform ation and does not supersede any previous understandings, commitments or agreements, whether written or oral. No amendment or modification of this Agreement will be effective unless communicated verbally between representatives of both parties and confirmed in writing.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'entire agreement' to 'only a partial understanding' introduces an in-text contradiction. Also, the agreement mentions that it supersedes other agreements 'supersedes any previous understandings' to 'does not supersede any previous understandings'. Finally, the agreement is effective when 'made in writing' to 'communicated verbally...confirmed in writing'. This contradiction creates uncertainty by failing to be the only agreement. This affects how outside the contract terms are interpreted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.6 Entire Agreement – This Agreement represents only a partial understanding between the parties with regard to the Con fidential Inform ation and does not supersede any previous understandings, commitments or agreements, whether written or oral. No amendment or modification of this Agreement will be effective unless communicated verbally between representatives of both parties and confirmed in writing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original intent of the 'Entire Agreement' clause. The original text states the agreement represents the 'entire agreement' and 'supersedes any previous understandings', while the changed text states it represents 'only a partial understanding' and 'does not supersede any previous understandings'. Additionally, the original text requires amendments to be 'made in writing', while the changed text allows them to be 'communicated verbally...confirmed in writing'. Since the contradicted text is a copy of the changed text, the change creates an obvious and irreconcilable in-text contradiction by fundamentally altering the nature and enforceability of the agreement and how amendments are handled. This adheres to the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.
